title: armeniaca × leiocarpa
intro: Armeniaca × leiocarpa, also known as apricot kernel tree, is a hybrid plant derived from the combination of Prunus armeniaca and Prunus pseudocerasus. The plant's medicinal properties are primarily attributed to its kernel, which contains amygdalin, a compound that has been used to treat various health issues. The amygdalin in Armeniaca × leiocarpa has been reported to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, potentially providing health benefits such as reducing cancer cell growth and alleviating symptoms of arthritis. Armeniaca × leiocarpa is often used in herbal preparations, including tinctures and extracts, which are consumed orally to harness its medicinal properties.
